description.para: If interrupt is enabled, and the application wants to be notified about the completion of a transfer, the application should register its call back function to the driver.
The call back function is registered through the transfer submission.

parameterdescription.para: is the call back reference passed in by application. A NULL pointer is acceptable.
is the interrupt mask regarding this completion
is the pointer to number of BDs this handler should handle, for simple transfer, it is ignored.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XAxiCdma_CallBackFn)(void *CallBackRef, u32 IrqMask, int *NumBdPtr)
memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_CallBackFn

briefdescription.para: Minimum byte alignment requirement for descriptors to satisfy both hardware/software needs.

description.para: This register reports status of a DMA channel, including idle state, errors, and interrupts
These masks are shared by XAXICDMA_CR_OFFSET register and XAXICDMA_SR_OFFSET register

briefdescription.para: All interrupts for simple only mode.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and shift for delay counter and coalescing counter

description.para: These masks are shared by XAXICDMA_CR_OFFSET register and XAXICDMA_SR_OFFSET register
The first 8 words are used by hardware.
Cache operations are required for words used by hardware to enforce data consistency. All words after the 8th word are for software use only.

briefdescription.para: BD specific bytes to be cleared.
Total number of words for one BD.

detaileddescription.para: Buffer descriptor, shared by hardware and software The structure of the BD is as the following, however, the user never directly accesses the fields in the BD. The following shows the fields inside a BD:
u32 NextBdPtr; u32 NextBdPtrMsb; u32 SrcAddr; u32 SrcAddrMsb; u32 DstAddr; u32 DstAddrMsb; int Length; u32 Status; u32 PhysAddr; u32 IsLite; u32 HasDRE; u32 WordLen; u32 Addrlen;

memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_ConfigTable

briefdescription.para: This function gets the status on error bits.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The error bits in the status register. Zero indicates no errors.

detaileddescription.para: Current transfer will finish gracefully. However, all queued SG transfers that have not started will be flushed from the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether the hardware reset is done.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

listitem.para: 1 if the reset has finished successfully
0 if the reset is not done

detaileddescription.para: It should be called before any other function calls to the driver.
It sets up the driver according to the hardware build. It resets the hardware at the end.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance that is working on
is the pointer to the hardware configuration structure
is the virtual address of the hardware instance. If address translation is not in use, please use the physical address

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if word length is less than 4
XST_FAILURE for reset failure

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether the hardware is doing transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

listitem.para: 1 if the hardware is doing a transfer
0 if the hardware is idle

briefdescription.para: This function configures KeyHole Write/Read Feature.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is WRITE/READ Select is the option to enable (TRUE) or disable (FALSE).

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_DEVICE_BUSY when transfer is in progress
XST_NO_FEATURE when not configured with feature

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiCdma *InstancePtr, UINTPTR SrcAddr, UINTPTR DstAddr, int Length, XAxiCdma_CallBackFn SimpleCallBack, void *CallBackRef)
memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_SimpleTransfer

briefdescription.para: This function does one simple transfer submission.

detaileddescription.para:
It checks in the following sequence:
if engine is busy, cannot submit
if software is still handling the completion of the previous simple transfer, cannot submit
if engine is in SG mode and cannot switch to simple mode, cannot submit

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the driver instance
is the address of the source buffer
is the address of the destination buffer
is the length of the transfer
is the callback function for the simple transfer
is the callback reference pointer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success of submission
XST_FAILURE for submission failure, maybe caused by: Another simple transfer is still going . Another SG transfer is still going
XST_INVALID_PARAM if: Length out of valid range [1: XAXICDMA_MAX_TRANSFER_LEN] Or, address not aligned when DRE is not built in

simplesect.para: Only set the callback function if using interrupt to signal the completion.If used in polling mode, please set the callback function to be NULL.

briefdescription.para: This function tries to set the interrupt coalescing threshold counter and the delay counter.

detaileddescription.para: If to set only one of the counters, set the value of the other counter to be XAXICDMA_COALESCE_NO_CHANGE.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the coalescing threshold to set to, the valid range is 1 to XAXICDMA_COALESCE_MAX.
is the delay timeout counter to set to, the valid range is 0 to XAXICDMA_DELAY_MAX. Setting a value of 0 disables the delay interrupt.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_FAILURE if hardware is in invalid state, for example, reset failed
XST_INVALID_PARAM if one of the counters is not in the valid range

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current setting of the interrupt coalescing threshold counter and the delay counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the return value for the coalescing counter setting
is the return value for the delay counter setting

simplesect.para: A zero coalescing threshold indicates invalid results

briefdescription.para: This function dumps the registers of this DMA instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function enables interrupts specified by the Mask.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not in the mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the mask for the interrupts to be enabled

briefdescription.para: This function gets the mask for the interrupts that are currently enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The bit mask for the interrupts that are currently enabled

briefdescription.para: This function disables interrupts specified by the Mask.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts that are not in the mask are not affected.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the mask for the interrupts to be disabled

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver, it handles all the interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: For the completion of a transfer that has a callback function, the callback function is called.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference pointer passed to the interrupt registration function. It will be a pointer to the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: If one transfer does not have all its submitted BDs completed successfully,then a reset is needed to clean up the mess left by that transfer.Otherwise, the wrong interrupt callback maybe called for the following transfers. However, if you always use the same interrupt callback for all the transfers, and you are the only user of the DMA engine, then you do not have to worry about this.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates how many BDs can be built using given number of bytes of memory, according to alignment provided.

parameterdescription.para: is the preferred alignment for the BDs
is the number of bytes of memory to build BDs with
is the buffer address allocated for the BDs. This is to check the alignment of the buffer to make sure the the buffer is aligned to the BD alignment. An invalid buffer address results in 0.

simplesect.para: The number of BDs can be built. 0 means buffer address is not valid.
The application is responsible to align the buffer before pass it to this function.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates how much memory is needed to build requested number of BDs.

parameterdescription.para: is the preferred alignment for the BDs
is the number of BDs to be built

simplesect.para: The number of bytes of memory needed to build the BDs

briefdescription.para: This function gets the total number of BDs in the BD ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The total number of BDs for this instance

briefdescription.para: This function gets the number of free BDs.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The total number of free BDs for this instance

briefdescription.para: This function grabs a copy of the current BD pointer from the hardware.

detaileddescription.para: It is normally used to prepare for the hardware reset. The snapshot of the current BD pointer should be reloaded once the reset is done.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

briefdescription.para: This function grabs a copy of the current BD pointer from the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on

simplesect.para: The BD pointer in CDESC register

briefdescription.para: This function gets the next BD of the current BD on the BD ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the current BD

simplesect.para: The next BD on the ring from the current BD, NULL if passed in BdPtr not valid.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the previous BD of the current BD on the BD ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the current BD

simplesect.para: The previous BD on the ring from the current BD

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiCdma *InstancePtr, UINTPTR PhysAddr, UINTPTR VirtAddr, u32 Alignment, int BdCount)
memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_BdRingCreate

briefdescription.para: This function creates the BD ring for the driver instance.

detaileddescription.para: If a BD ring pre-exist of this ring, the previous ring is lost.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the physical address of the memory for the BD ring
is the virtual address of the memory for the BD ring
is the alignment for the BDs
is the number of BDs to create in the ring

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM for invalid parameter
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR for invalid memory region
XST_FAILURE if the hardware build is simple mode only

simplesect.para: For a system that has flat memory layout, then the PhysAddr and the VirtAddr are the same.

briefdescription.para: This function clones all BDs in the BD ring to be the same as the given BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the BD to be copied from

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_DMA_SG_NO_LIST if there is no BD ring
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the hardware is running
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR is the BD ring is still in use

briefdescription.para: This function requests number of BDs from the BD ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the number of BDs to request
is the pointer to the set of BDs returned

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if requests non-positive number of BDs
XST_FAILURE if not enough free BDs available

briefdescription.para: This function tries to free the number of BDs back to the ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the number of BDs to return
is the set of BDs to be returned

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if to free non-positive number of BDs
XST_FAILURE if BD ring management shows an error

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiCdma *InstancePtr, int NumBd, XAxiCdma_Bd *BdSetPtr, XAxiCdma_CallBackFn CallBackFn, void *CallBackRef)
memberdef.name: XAxiCdma_BdRingToHw

briefdescription.para: This function tries to enqueue the number of BDs to the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the number of BDs to enqueue
is the set of BDs to be enqueued
is the callback function for this transfer, NULL is fine
is the callback reference pointer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if enqueues negative number of BDs or zero transfer len
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if BD ring management has a problem
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if the interrupt handler array is full
XST_FAILURE for: Hardware is in invalid state, for example, reset failed Or, the hardware build is simple mode only

briefdescription.para: This function tries to retrieve completed BDs from the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the maximum number of completed BDs to retrieve
is the set of completed BDs

briefdescription.para: This function returns the BDs back to the free pool of the BD ring.

parameterdescription.para: is the driver instance we are working on
is the number of BDs to free
is the set of BDs to be freed

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if number of BDs is negative
XST_DMA_SG_LIST_ERROR if the BD ring management has a problem

briefdescription.para: This function set the Current BD ptr of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the Next BD ptr
is the valud of Current BD ptr

briefdescription.para: This function set the Tail BD ptr of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the Next BD ptr
Bd is the value of Tail BD ptr

briefdescription.para: This function clears the content of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to be cleared

briefdescription.para: This function clones the content from the template BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to be cloned to
is the pointer to the BD to be cloned from

briefdescription.para: This function gets the next BD pointer of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the next BD ptr

simplesect.para: The next BD ptr of the BD.

briefdescription.para: This function set the Next BD ptr of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the Next BD ptr
is the valud of Next BD ptr

briefdescription.para: This function gets the completion status of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the status

simplesect.para: The status bits of the BD.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the status bits of the BD.

detaileddescription.para: The status bits include the completion bit as well as the error bits.
This is the only function that needs to be called to clear a completed BD. All other fields (except reserved words) need to be re-assigned before re-submission.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to clear the status

briefdescription.para: This function sets the source address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the source address
is the source address of the buffer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if buffer address set successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if buffer address is not aligned and hardware build has no DRE or in lite mode

briefdescription.para: This function gets the source address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the source address

simplesect.para: The source address of the buffer that this BD describes

briefdescription.para: This function sets the destination address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the destination address
is the destination address of the buffer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if buffer address set successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if buffer address is not aligned and hardware build has no DRE or in lite mode

briefdescription.para: This function gets the destination address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the destination address

simplesect.para: The destination address of the buffer that this BD describes

briefdescription.para: This function sets the length of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the length
is the length of the buffer

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for success
XST_INVALID_PARAM if invalid length: Length value out of valid range

simplesect.para: One case that will cause hardware failure is not covered here: If the start address is not at page offset 0, and the length plus the offset crosses the page boundary, this will cause slave error if the hardware is built in lite mode.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the length of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the length

simplesect.para: The length of the buffer that this BD describes

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Physical address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to set the physical address
is the physical address of the BD

briefdescription.para: This function sets the is lite mode field of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to work on
is the value for whether hardware is in lite mode

briefdescription.para: This function sets the has DRE field of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to work on
is the value for whether hardware has DRE built in

briefdescription.para: This function sets the word length field of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to work on
is the length of word in bytes

briefdescription.para: This function sets the maximum transfer length field of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to work on
is the maximum transfer length in bytes

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Physical address of the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the physical address

simplesect.para: The physical address of the BD

briefdescription.para: This function dumps the BD, it is a debug utility.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the BD to get the physical address
is the base virtual address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XAxiCdma_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: is the base virtual address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiCdma_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: The implementation of the API of Xilinx CDMA engine.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
This is a guide on how to move from using the XPS Central DMA driver, dmacentral, to use xaxidma driver.
The implementation of the interrupt related API.
Header file for utility functions shared by driver files.
Hardware definition file.
Provide a template for user to define their own hardware settings.
Implementation for support on Scatter Gather (SG) transfers.
The API definition for the Buffer Descriptor (BD).
The implementation for the Buffer Descriptor (BD) API functions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 04/18/10 First release 2.01a rkv 01/25/11 Replaced with "\r\n" in place on "\n\r" in printf statements Changed XAxiCdma_CfgInitialize to use EffectiveAddress. 2.02a srt 01/18/13 Added support for Key Hole feature (CR: 687217). 4.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XAxiCdma_CfgInitialize API. 4.7 rsp 11/29/19 Fix XAxiCdma_SimpleTransfer documentation for BTT.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 04/08/10 First release 2.01a rkv 01/25/11 Replaced with "\r\n" in place of "\n\r" in printf statements

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 04/16/10 First release

detaileddescription.para: It includes the implementation of the BD ring API. There is only one BD ring per DMA engine.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 04/18/10 First release 2.01a rkv 01/25/11 Replaced with "\r\n" in place on "\n\r" in printf statements 4.3 mi 09/21/16 Fixed compilation warnings 4.5 rsp 07/04/18 Fixed cppcheck warning

detaileddescription.para: If using XPS, XPS will automatically generate this file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release 4.5 rsp 07/04/18 Sync XAxiCdma_Config initializer fields

detaileddescription.para: It defines the register interface and Buffer Descriptor (BD) definitions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 04/08/10 First release 2.02a srt 01/18/13 Added support for Key Hole feature (CR: 687217). 4.7 rsp 11/29/19 Remove hard-coded XAXICDMA_MAX_TRANSFER_LEN. In driver tcl derive it based on IP version.

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt handler is also implemented here.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 07/08/10 First release 2.01a rkv 01/25/11 Replaced with "\r\n" in place on "\n\r" in printf statements 2.03a srt 04/13/13 Removed Warnings (CR 705006).

detaileddescription.para: The AXI Central DMA engine is to replace the XPS Central DMA engine using the AXI bus interface.

The AXI Central DMA hardware is different from the XPS Central DMA engine. The APS Central DMA engine only supports simple mode transfer, where only one buffer transfer can be sumitted to the hardware each time.

The AXI Central DMA engine, on the other hand, also supports scatter gather transfers, where each submission to the hardware can have multiple buffers. Each transfer buffer is defined by a buffer descriptor (BD).

The AXI Central DMA engine can be configured at build time to be in simple mode only, which is similar to the XPS Central DMA. It can also be configured as hybrid mode, where scatter gather mode is also supported.
The difference from the driver API level is as the following:

listitem.para: User defined interrupt handler is no longer directly connected to the interrupt controller. Instead, it is registered as a call back function when the submit the transfer:

para.preformatted: XAxiCdma_SimpleTransfer(InstancePtr, TxAddr, RxAddr, Length, CallBackFn, CallBackRef);

detaileddescription.para: Please refer to example_simple_intr.c to see how it is used.

listitem.para: Transfer submission API is different:
Before: XDmaCentral_Transfer(...)
Now: XAxiCdma_SimpleTransfer(...)

detaileddescription.para: Note that arguments list is also different. Please see example_simple_poll.c or example_simple_intr.c for how to submit simple transfers.

listitem.para: Polling for transfer completion is different:
Now:

detaileddescription.para: The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xaxivdma_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a jz 08/16/10 First release
